skill.name: istio-traffic-management
skill.description: Configure Istio traffic management including routing, load balancing, circuit breakers, and canary deployments. Use for service mesh traffic policies and progressive delivery.
skill.summary: Create practical Istio routing, resilience, ingress, and rollout configurations.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found these are false positives from Markdown fences, Istio resource names, and official documentation links. The skill is a documentation-only guide with sample YAML and non-destructive istioctl diagnostic commands. No prompt injection, malware intent, credential access, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command findings at the template sections are Markdown code fences for YAML examples, not Ruby or shell execution. These examples are inert documentation unless a user manually applies them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visible Markdown code block delimiters and YAML examples. There is no executable Ruby, shell interpolation, or automatic command invocation in these sections.

low_findings.title: Documented Istio Diagnostic Commands Require Operator Review
low_findings.description: The skill includes istioctl commands for analyzing routes, endpoints, and proxy logging. They are legitimate operational commands, but users should run them only against intended clusters.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and limited to Istio diagnostics. They can expose cluster routing information or change debug logging level, so they are a low operational risk.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Istio Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography alerts map to ordinary Istio names, YAML resource kinds, and documentation text. No cryptographic algorithm selection or insecure hash usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found Istio traffic-management documentation at each cited area. No hashing, encryption, TLS downgrade, or weak cipher configuration was present.

low_findings.title: Official Documentation Links Are Benign Network References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to official Istio documentation pages. They are reference links and do not send data or execute network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are static Markdown links to istio.io documentation. No code path fetches these URLs or transmits user data.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Is a Header Name Example
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert maps to an x-user-id header used for consistent hashing. It is not host discovery, port scanning, or environment probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is inside an Istio load balancing example and only names an HTTP header. There is no command or logic that gathers system information.

content.value_statement: Istio traffic changes are risky when routing, retries, and rollout rules are built from memory. This skill provides practical templates for controlled service mesh traffic management.
actual_capabilities: Provides VirtualService and DestinationRule examples for service routing.
Shows weighted canary traffic splits for progressive delivery.
Documents circuit breaker, retry, timeout, and outlier detection settings.
Includes ingress gateway, traffic mirroring, and fault injection examples.
Lists istioctl commands for route, endpoint, and proxy diagnostics.
limitations: Does not validate manifests against a live Kubernetes cluster.
Does not choose production values for every service automatically.
Requires users to adapt hosts, namespaces, subsets, and labels.
Does not replace Istio security policy or mTLS configuration guidance.

use_cases.title: Plan a Canary Rollout
use_cases.description: Create a weighted routing plan that sends a small traffic share to a new service version.

use_cases.title: Add Resilience Controls
use_cases.description: Define timeout, retry, and circuit breaker settings for an unreliable service dependency.

use_cases.title: Debug Mesh Routing
use_cases.description: Use Istio diagnostic commands to inspect active routes, endpoints, and proxy behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Routing
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Istio VirtualService and DestinationRule for service reviews with versions v1 and v2.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Canary Split
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an Istio canary rollout for my-service with 95 percent stable traffic and 5 percent canary traffic.

prompt_templates.title: Add Resilience Policies
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend Istio timeout, retry, connection pool, and outlier detection settings for a latency-sensitive API.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Traffic Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Istio traffic plan for rollout safety, failure isolation, and observability gaps before production use.

output_examples.input: I need a safe canary plan for a new checkout service version.
output: A recommended stable and canary traffic split.
Required subset labels and destination policy notes.
A short rollout checklist with monitoring points.

output_examples.input: My service has intermittent 503 errors during downstream failures.
output: Circuit breaker and outlier detection guidance.
Warnings about retry storms and cascading failures.

output_examples.input: Traffic is not reaching the expected service subset.
output: Diagnostic steps using Istio route and endpoint inspection.
Common causes involving labels, hosts, and gateway binding.
A focused checklist for comparing desired and effective routes.

best_practices: Start with simple routing rules before adding retries, mirroring, or fault injection.
Set explicit timeouts and bounded retries for every critical service path.
Verify subset labels and observe metrics before increasing canary traffic.
anti_patterns: Do not mirror production traffic into systems that cannot safely receive it.
Do not increase retries without checking latency and downstream capacity.
Do not apply sample hosts, namespaces, or TLS secret names unchanged.

faq.question: Does this skill apply Istio resources automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples that users must review and apply in their own environment.

faq.question: Can it help with canary deployments?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes weighted routing examples and guidance for gradual traffic shifts.

faq.question: Does it cover circuit breakers?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents connection pool limits, outlier detection, and related resilience settings.

faq.question: Is this a full Istio security guide?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on traffic management, not authorization policies or mTLS hardening.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What should I change before production use?
faq.answer: Update hosts, namespaces, labels, ports, gateway names, and rollout percentages for your cluster.
